blocks:
Ayahs

لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدِ (۱)
I swear by this town, (1)
وَأَنتَ حِلٌّۢ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدِ (۲)
as you reside in this town; (2)
وَوَالِدٍ وَمَا وَلَدَ (۳)
[and] by the father and him whom he begot: (3)
لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ فِى كَبَدٍ (٤)
certainly We created man in travail. (4)
أَيَحْسَبُ أَن لَّن يَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ أَحَدٌ (٥)
Does he suppose that no one will ever have power over him? (5)
يَقُولُ أَهْلَكْتُ مَالًا لُّبَدًا (٦)
He says, ‘I have squandered immense wealth.’ (6)
أَيَحْسَبُ أَن لَّمْ يَرَهُۥٓ أَحَدٌ (۷)
Does he suppose that no one sees him? (7)

Key Points

The phrase "لا اُقسم" (I swear not) has been used three times in the Qur'an, and exegetes have interpreted it in two ways. One group considers the letter lā (لا) redundant and interprets it as (I swear). And another group translates it as (I swear not), meaning that the matter is so clear that there is no need for an oath.

The intent of al-Balad (the City) is Mecca, which was respected even before Islam. Mecca is the secure Sanctuary of Allah and the first House established for humanity.

The phrase "و انت حلّ" (and you are free/lawful in this city) has been interpreted in several ways, one meaning of which we included in the translation. The second meaning is that the people of Mecca deem your humiliation lawful therein, so I do not swear by a city where they consider your humiliation lawful.(53) And the third meaning is that your hand is open concerning this city, and during the Conquest of Mecca, you may take any decision you wish regarding the opponents.

Kabad (كَبَد) means hardship, and Labad (لَبَد) means dense wool. However, some have taken Kabad to mean upright and moderate.(54)

That man has been created amidst suffering and hardship means that worldly enjoyments are mixed with pain and toil. "لقد خلقنا الانسان فى كبد" (We have certainly created man in hardship). Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The world is a house surrounded by afflictions. "دار بالبلاء محفوفة" (A house encompassed by tribulation).(55)

Although some exegetes, due to the phrase "هذا البلد" (this City) which is Mecca, have considered the intent of "والد و ما ولد" (the parent and what he begot) to be Prophet Abraham and his son Prophet Ishmael, yet, as stated in Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, the intent is Prophet Adam and the saints (Awliyā') from his progeny.

It is mentioned in a hadith that the speaker of the phrase "اهلكت مالاً لبداً" (I have destroyed abundant wealth) was Amr ibn Abd Wudd during the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq), who said: I wasted a great fortune fighting Islam, but I did not succeed.(56)

53) This meaning is mentioned in a hadith in Bihar, Vol. 24, p. 284. 54) Lisan al-Arab (quoted from Tafsir Rahnama). 55) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 226. 56) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Lands can possess sanctity, and the city of Mecca has been respected and sworn by since ancient times. "لا اقسم بهذا البلد" (I swear by this City).
  2. Repetition is sometimes a sign of greatness and attention. "هذا البلد....هذا البلد" (This City... this City).
  3. The value of lands lies in the value of their inhabitants. "و انت حِلّ بهذا البلد" (And you are free/lawful in this City). (The oath by Mecca is due to the presence of the Prophet).
  4. In the context of offspring, there is no difference between a daughter or a son. "و ما ولد" (and what he begot).
  5. Human life is situated amidst struggles and hardships. "فى كبد" (in hardship).
  6. Any wealth not spent in the path of truth leads to regret. "اهلكت مالاً لبدا" (I have destroyed abundant wealth).
  7. Man is under the dominion of Divine power and knowledge. "ایحسب ان لن یقدر علیه احد... ایحسب ان لم یره احد" (Does he think that no one has power over him... Does he think that no one has seen him?).
  8. The result of any calculation that lacks God's presence is futility and loss. "ایحسب... ایحسب" (Does he think... Does he think).
  9. What meaning does arrogance before God hold? "ایحسب ان لن یقدر علیه احد" (Does he think that no one has power over him?).
  10. Those who do not follow the correct path in spending (infaq), or whose expenditure is inappropriate, or whose intention is not sincere, or is accompanied by hypocrisy, showing off, and falsehood, will be ashamed and regretful in the future. "اهلكت مالا لُبداً" (I have destroyed abundant wealth).

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Balad

Surah Al-Balad has twenty verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is derived from the first verse, and the intent of al-Balad (the City) is the city of Mecca, by which Allah has sworn.

The Surah begins with an oath and, by referring to the system of human procreation and lineage, introduces life in this world as being accompanied by effort and toil. It then censures the heedless and arrogant people, points to the valuable blessings that Allah has bestowed upon humanity, and states that the way to show gratitude for these blessings is by attending to the deprived and serving the orphans. Of course, in response to these blessings, people divide into two groups: a grateful group, which will have a blessed and auspicious outcome, and an ungrateful group, for whom a grim and difficult end awaits.

Ayahs

أَلَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُۥ عَيْنَيْنِ (۸)
Have We not made for him two eyes, (8)
وَلِسَانًا وَشَفَتَيْنِ (۹)
a tongue, and two lips, (9)
وَهَدَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلنَّجْدَيْنِ (۱٠)
and shown him the two paths [of good and evil]? (10)
فَلَا ٱقْتَحَمَ ٱلْعَقَبَةَ (۱۱)
Yet he has not embarked upon the uphill task. (11)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلْعَقَبَةُ (۱۲)
And what will show you what is the uphill task? (12)
فَكُّ رَقَبَةٍ (۱۳)
[It is] the freeing of a slave, (13)
أَوْ إِطْعَـٰمٌ فِى يَوْمٍ ذِى مَسْغَبَةٍ (۱٤)
or feeding, during days of [general] starvation, (14)
يَتِيمًا ذَا مَقْرَبَةٍ (۱٥)
an orphan among relatives (15)
أَوْ مِسْكِينًا ذَا مَتْرَبَةٍ (۱٦)
or a needy man in desolation, (16)

Key Points

57) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 163. 58) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 59) Abasa, 24. 60) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan. 61) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The best way to know Allah is through careful study and reflection upon His wise creations. «الم نجعل له...»

2- Awaken dormant consciences with appropriate questions. «الم نجعل له...»

3- All human beings recognize good and evil through intellect, innate nature (Fitrah), or the guidance of the Prophets, their successors (Awsiya), and scholars. «هدیناه النّجدین»

4- Fulfilling duty and obligation is like traversing a steep path (a pass). «العقبة»

5- Affluent and comfort-seeking individuals who evade work and the acceptance of responsibility are criticized by the Quran. «فلا اقتحم العقبه»

6- Man does not comprehend spiritual matters without divine warnings. «و ما ادریك ما العقبة»

7- Freeing slaves and captives is at the forefront of good deeds. «فكّ رقبة»

8- Generalizing is not always useful; sometimes specific examples and instances must be named. «فكّ رقبة... اطعام»

9- To stir people's emotions and awaken consciences, highlight the suffering (hardships). «مسغبة... مقربة... متربة»

10- To encourage people toward good deeds, first remind them of Allah's favors. «عینین - لساناً شفتین - یتیما ذا مقربة»

11- Islam emphasizes and insists upon addressing the material issues of the deprived class and the necessity of solving their problems. «یتیماً مسكیناً»

12- Wealth is spent, but the important thing is the path of expenditure. «اهلكت مالاً لُبداً... فلا اقتحم العقبة... فكّ رقبة... او اطعام...»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلْمَرْحَمَةِ (۱۷)
while being one of those who have faith and enjoin one another to patience, and enjoin one another to compassion. (17)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْمَيْمَنَةِ (۱۸)
They are the People of the Right Hand. (18)
وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا هُمْ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْمَشْـَٔمَةِ (۱۹)
But those who defy Our signs, they are the People of the Left Hand. (19)
عَلَيْهِمْ نَارٌ مُّؤْصَدَةٌۢ (۲٠)
A closed Fire will be [imposed] upon them. (20)

Key Points

The phrase «ثمّ كان من الّذین آمنوا» (Then he was among those who believed) has been interpreted in two ways: A: Good deeds, such as freeing a slave and feeding the hungry, are a prelude (or foundation) for inclining toward faith and enjoining others to good deeds. B: The status and value of faith are higher than feeding the hungry and freeing a slave, hence it is separated by the word «ثمّ» (then). (62)

«میمنة» (Maymanah) derives from yumn and yamin, meaning blessedness and auspiciousness. Aṣḥāb al-Maymanah (Companions of the Right) means those whom permanent blessing accompanies and adheres to. It seems that the Companions of the Right are those whose record of deeds will be given into their right hands.

«مشئمة» (Mash'amah) derives from shūm, meaning inauspiciousness. The word «مؤصدة» (Mu'ṣadah) derives from īṣād, meaning to close a door and secure it firmly.

The word «تواصوا» (Tawāṣaw—enjoined one another) is used four times in the Quran: once as «تواصوا بالحق» (enjoined one another to the truth), twice as «تواصوا بالصبر» (enjoined one another to patience), and once as «تواصوا بالمرحمة» (enjoined one another to mercy).

The repetition of the word «تواصوا» in a single Ayah is either for emphasis or because patience (ṣabr) and mercy (marḥamah) are each independent duties, and neglecting one does not eliminate the basis for the other. (63)

No one is needless of counsel and encouragement.

The word «تواصوا», which appears in the Tafā‘ul form (reciprocal action), means that enjoining good deeds must be mutual and a general, public movement, not one-sided.

In the previous verses, it was enjoined that we attend to the orphan and the needy; in this Ayah, He says that we should also enjoin others to attend to them and show mercy.

Although «تواصوا بالمرحمة» (enjoined one another to mercy) is usually used alongside «آمنوا» (they believed) and «عملوا الصالحات» (they did righteous deeds), here, «تواصوا بالصبر و تواصوا بالمرحمة» (enjoined one another to patience and enjoined one another to mercy) has replaced «عملوا الصالحات», as if these two injunctions are themselves righteous deeds.

Islam pays full attention not only to good deeds but also to the beliefs and motivations of individuals; meaning, in addition to freeing a slave and feeding the poor, the individual must also be a person of faith.

How many are those who are willing to provide financial aid and humanitarian services but are unwilling to accept the school of thought, the path, and the sound argument of the truth! Such individuals are also stranded and have not crossed the steep path (the ‘aqabah). Therefore, the Quran states: the condition for crossing the steep path is financial services and aid to the captive, the needy, and the orphan, provided that it is accompanied by the spirit of accepting the truth and faith. «ثم كان من الّذین آمنوا»

Perhaps the intent behind the covered Hellfire is what is mentioned in another Ayah: «لهم من جهنّم مهاد و من فوقهم غواش» (For them there will be beds of Hellfire and coverings above them). (64)

62) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 63) Tafsir-e Rāhnamā. 64) Al-A‘rāf, 41.

Messages

1- Faith is a prerequisite for the acceptance of good deeds. «ثم كان من الّذین آمنوا»

2- All Muslims must enjoin one another to patience and mercy. «آمنوا و تواصوا»

3- Knowing is not enough; speaking and listening are also effective. «تواصوا»

4- The best good deeds (ma‘rūf) that must be enjoined are patience and mercy. «تواصوا بالصبر و تواصوا بالمرحمة»

5- After self-purification, one must invite others to good deeds. «تواصوا بالمرحمة»

6- Gratitude for the blessing of the eye is seeing the divine signs (Ayat); gratitude for the blessing of the tongue is enjoining others. «تواصوا»

7- Faith, patience, and affection are sources of blessing. «اولئك اصحاب المیمنة»

8- Whoever covers the face of truth and mercy with disbelief and stinginess will be placed in a covered fire on the Day of Judgment. «علیهم نار مؤصدة»

9- Believers who succeed in feeding the hungry and freeing a slave are a source of goodness and blessing for society, and they have also built up their Hereafter. What greater auspiciousness and blessing is there than this? «اصحاب المیمنة»

10- Every task reaches fruition through forbearance and steadfastness; therefore, enjoining patience precedes every other matter. «تواصوا بالصبر»

11- The source of all inauspiciousness and impurity reverts to disbelief and concealing the truth. «والّذین كفروا... هم اصحاب المشئمة»
